name: Oatmeal Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 quart low fat buttermilk (or skim milk soured with 1 tablespoon lemon juice per cup)
Egg substitute to equal four eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the dry ingredients: In a large bowl, mix together the oatmeal, flour, baking soda, and salt.
2. Mix in the wet ingredients: Add the buttermilk (or soured milk mixture) and egg substitute to the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined; the batter will be relatively thin.
3. Cook the pancakes: Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Spray with cooking oil if desired. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter per pancake onto the hot surface.
4. Flip and cook: Once the edges start to look set and there are bubbles on the surface, gently flip the pancakes using a spatula. Cook for another minute or until golden brown on both sides.
This recipe makes a large batch, so consider halving it for 2-4 servings.
